task_completion.description: Navigate to the Sprint 1 backlog or board in the TailwindTraders project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Azure DevOps web interface is visible, showing the 'TailwindTraders' project with 'Sprint 1' selected in the Sprints or Backlogs view.

task_completion.description: Set realistic daily capacity for team members in Sprint 1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Capacity' tab for Sprint 1 is open, and numerical values (e.g., hours per day) are entered in the 'Capacity per day' column for the listed team members.

task_completion.description: Reassign a lower-priority work item to Sprint 2.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A work item's details page or a backlog view shows its 'Iteration' field being changed from 'Sprint 1' to 'Sprint 2'. The item must not be a Priority 1 bug or high-priority user story.

task_completion.description: Add a comment to the first moved item explaining the reason for the move.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Discussion or History section of the first moved work item contains a newly typed and saved comment explaining why the item was deferred to Sprint 2.

task_completion.description: Reassign a second lower-priority work item to Sprint 2.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second work item's details page or backlog view shows its 'Iteration' field being changed from 'Sprint 1' to 'Sprint 2'. The item must not be a Priority 1 bug or high-priority user story.

task_completion.description: Add a comment to the second moved item explaining the reason for the move.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Discussion or History section of the second moved work item contains a newly typed and saved comment explaining why the item was deferred to Sprint 2.

task_completion.description: Ensure Priority 1 bugs and high-priority user stories remain in Sprint 1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sprint 1 backlog or taskboard shows that Priority 1 bugs and high-priority user stories are still present in the sprint and have not been reassigned.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Azure DevOps web interface rather than using terminal commands or API scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the web browser navigating the Azure DevOps portal, clicking UI elements, and filling out forms.

integrity.description: The agent typed contextually relevant comments rather than copy-pasting a generic placeholder.
integrity.visual_evidence: The comments added to the work items are contextually relevant to deferring the item due to capacity constraints, rather than just a pasted string from the prompt.

integrity.description: The agent actually saved the modifications to the work items in the system.
integrity.visual_evidence: The work item forms show the 'Save' or 'Save & Close' buttons being clicked, and the backlog reflects the updated iteration paths.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target project is 'TailwindTraders'. The initial sprint is 'Sprint 1', which contains 8 work items in the database (with the first 5 items summing to 37 story points). The destination sprint for deferred items is 'Sprint 2'.
